reviews.review_description: I just bought five licences for my students at the metaverse creator's course at a vocational school, Nagoya, Japan. I want to teach them full body movement and presence in VR. This app makes them get up on their feet and move :)
More fun than expected. Nice to move around and get some light exercise

Edited the review because it seems like this is dead and no longer supported by the developer. 

It's a nice game but it's too limited, nothing has been updated in ages, and again seems the devs don't care. 

My last thought it to update this to support the Artemis vr bow

Please don't leave bad reviews for games that are not meant for you. It's not a full fledge game. There is no story, and it is not just a tech demo. This is a game that is designed to keep you moving by aiming, ducking, weaving, crouching all trying to avoid anything coming at you FROM ALL AROUND YOU! 

Shooting boxes that shoot back at you, so many ninjas running at you, while some that throw ninja stars at you. Ticking mines that will detonate... you have to hit them in midair to be safe! All of these moving pieces make for an amazing workout. It's makes me feel like hawkeye in a training simulation. 

I am fortunate enough to have enough room to do room scale. I turn off my guardian, and I have a mat that if I step off it, then I have to backtrack.  I have been a major source of entertainment for my family as i flair around my room like a mad man. 

If you don't have enough room, then the garden Area has you standing in one spot but is more designed for crouches and lunges. I haven't used it often because I like the more constant motion. 

Keep up good work... and keep updating. I went back to it recently and like the new changes.

reviews.review_title: Good workout gameplay for a cheap game
reviews.review_description:
It’s not really a full VR game… more like a full-featured tech demo.  But the gameplay is fun and it gives my legs a great workout.  And it’s cheap!

The frequent squatting motions in the gameplay also stop my legs from getting sore from lengthy room-scale VR gaming sessions.

Holopoint is an Absolute VR Masterpiece!

I’ve battled my way through all 30 levels in Dojo mode, and I can say without a doubt—this game is absolutely beautiful! The intensity, the realism, and the full-body workout it delivers are next-level. Holopoint doesn’t just let you play—it pushes you to evolve, sharpen your reflexes, and level up in a way that feels incredibly rewarding.

It has become one of my go-to VR experiences every time I put on the headset. The combination of speed, precision, and sheer adrenaline makes it an absolute must-play for anyone looking for a challenge!

Thank you, Holopoint! I truly appreciate the work that went into making this game so incredible. 🙏🏻

It's overpriced for what little it does. You're in a dojo or garden. A wave starts. You shoot each target that spawns. You dodge the bullet that shoots back when you hit them. If you take too long to hit them, they explode and shoot a bullet at you.

So, basically, you shoot, then dodge slightly out of the way over and over.

They introduce a few new targets, like one that bounces before shooting you. And successive waves have more targets show up, making you shoot faster or dodge more.

And, that's all there is.

If that sounds boring to you, skip this game. But, there's a certain target demographic of folks (like me) that like games where we can switch our brain off after a long day, at work, and just do mild repetition that relies on action and reaction.

But, I feel like the content is austere for the price.

I also feel that there just could be a bit more to mix it up. Some repetitionn is ok. But this game is literally jist shoot, dodge, shoot, dodge. It gets old. And the dodging doesn't really work up a, sweat. You just move your head a bit. Meh.

So, game could be better for what it's trying to do. Feels like a, glorified tech demo. Get it on sale if you must.

Honestly use it for cardio, and it works fantastic. Though I keep getting ‘tracking lost’ issues, but figured out it’s cuz I’m turning my head (not keeping controllers in view) rather than my whole upper body or when checking over my shoulder. Really annoying but not sure how it can be fixed, if it can at all. More up to my side and how I move I feel like, but kinda takes away from the experience of having the thought of being able to move freely, especially once it gets faster paced in later levels as that’s where I run into the tracking issues most.

Otherwise, I try and play it at least once a day, level 1 to however far I get

I really like the game.  The gameplay is great, and the controls are very simple and intuitive.

I also like the game because it’s fairly humane.  I don't believe in guns and the concept of hurting people (even zombies).  This kind of game with bow/arrow is just abstract enough, and the opponents aren't so humanized.  (The humanoid opponents are armored, and 'shatter' when your arrow hits them.)  For this reason, Holopoint is really a wonderful game.

But I give this 4 stars because there's almost a kind of bug in the game where occasionally it seems impossible to nock one’s arrow.  This bug should either be fixed, or there should be a tutorial to explain how to properly nock/re-nock the arrow.  (There have been moments where I have been forced to drop my bow to pick it up again, and then I get shot too many times and I’m out.)

[I have now updated my review to 5 stars, because I have figured out the nocking problem.  Basically, you can nock with either the trigger button or the grasp button.  If you nock with the trigger button, of course you let go of the trigger button to shoot.  However, if you accidentally depress the grasp button *as you try to let go* of the trigger button, it won't shoot.  Ideally, the devs can help to remedy this.  It should be that whatever button you use to nock the arrow should also automatically shoot the arrow when let go, regardless of what other buttons are being pressed.  All this means that I have now decided to put *both* my index and middle finger on the grasp button to shoot.  ]

reviews.review_title: Really fun but lacking in content
reviews.review_description: This game is super fun and addicting, I play it a lot and it's an exhausting excellent way to get exercise. However, there is currently (from what I've found) only 1 area to play in which seems limited to the Steam version which has multiple. I'm aware this may be due to it being a Quest game, but I hope with the Quest 2 they are able to add more content to this otherwise extremely addicting and fun game.

Archery in vr should be fantastic .. but I find this game just doesn’t quite reach where it could. 

The archery mechanic is ok, but the game around it is just very average.

Ten out of ten would recommend to anyone who wants to workout but not hit the gym. Only down side is that you might fall back on a couch or something like that if you move around a lot while playing the game.

This game makes me feel like I'm Katniss Everdeen in the 2nd Hunger Games movie where she's training in that simulation room. It's very simple but it's very effective, and within minutes I'm sweaty. It's so much fun that it doesn't feel like exercise, but it's definitely exercise! The mechanics are quite good and it felt very natural very quickly.

You need quite a bit of room to play this game though, I felt like I was running into the guardian a lot.
Not a lot of variety and different levels but definitely worth $15.

reviews.review_title: Great game but could be improved!
reviews.review_description:
I‘m playing Holopoint since a few days and I love the japanese style of it, but I would love it even more if there would be some add-ons or DLC available ... I would love to have different rooms or even an outdoor space. Extra bows and different arrows would be great too, maybe as an reward after playing certain levels. 
But what I really would like to have is a Kyūdō Dojo for japanese archery. This would be perfect for meditative training and would add a „slow“ mode to the game.

UPDATE: I emailed the company about the below issue I had, and they have said they will add a floor boundary system to the next update cycle!! How's that for a great company! 4 to 5 stars from me!!

Original review:

This game is great, with one small issue for me. There's a lot of movement needed, and even in a biggish size area, above what is recommended, i still feel uncomfortable not knowing where my boundary is, so ...

NOTE TO DEVS: could you add an option to show some sort of floor boundary in game (the way 'Thrill of the Fight' does this is excellent, and makes you feel safe you're not about to punch a wall even in the most frenetic of bouts) I realize this would upset your lovely clean Dojo, but could be set as an option, with varying brightness, for those that would swap a bit of Feng Shui for the comfort of not worrying we're about to hit a wall.

For me, this would make the game six stars, great fun and great aerobics too.

reviews.review_title: I guess good for the money....
reviews.review_description:
Needs more levels and more environments, maybe let us change the music and colors of targets
Maybe have more bows and arrows
Then it's 5 stars
For now it's 4

This is one of the most fun games I’ve played while having an oculus. I’m a hunger games fanatic so this is perfect for me, I feel like I’m in the training scene. I hope they update this game in the future and make it even more amazing!!

This was super fun, but I gotta warn you it is a work out. I was definitely not expecting the targets to shoot back at me but it definitely added to the experience. 

I feel like Katniss Everdeen when I play this.
I finally get to pretend like I’m Legolas from Lord of The Rings now my inner child is pleased. lol

developer_response.body: We are glad you enjoyed it and thank you for the positive review! If you have any feedback feel free to reach out at Feedback@AlzanStudios.com
